title: Miltoniopsis × bleui
taxonomy: Miltoniopsis × bleui is classified as an orchid species in the genus Miltoniopsis, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a hybrid orchid, specifically a cross between Miltoniopsis warscewiczii and Miltoniopsis x bayeri. This classification places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Miltoniopsis is primarily comprised of species native to Central and South America. As a hybrid orchid, Miltoniopsis × bleui exhibits characteristics from its parent species.

intro: Miltoniopsis × bleui, commonly known as Bleu Miltonia or Bleu Pansy Orchid, is a hybrid orchid plant species that belongs to the Miltonia genus. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The bioactive constituents of Miltoniopsis × bleui include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Miltoniopsis × bleui is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make cough syrups and expectorants to aid in the treatment of respiratory conditions.
